title: Change Manifest Schema
description: Schema for documenting code changes made by the implementer subagent. This artifact is passed to the auditor for compliance verification. Designed for inter-agent communication within the implement command.

objective_id.description: ID of the objective being implemented (e.g., '1.1' for secondary objective 1 of main objective 1).
objective_id.$comment: Must match the objective ID from the logbook. Format: 'N' for main or 'N.M' for secondary.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when implementation was completed (ISO 8601).
timestamp.$comment: Set when implementer finishes. Used for tracking and logging.

files_created.description: List of file paths that were created. Relative to project root.

files_modified.description: List of file paths that were modified. Relative to project root.
files_modified.$comment: Include full relative path. Only list files with actual changes.

files_deleted.description: List of file paths that were deleted. Relative to project root.
files_deleted.$comment: Rarely used. Include if objective required removing obsolete code.

patterns_applied.description: List of patterns that were applied during implementation.
patterns_applied.$comment: Examples: 'BaseDTO inheritance', 'Repository pattern', 'Decorator pattern'. Helps auditor understand intent.

rules_followed.description: IDs of project rules that were explicitly followed during implementation.
rules_followed.$comment: Reference rule IDs from project_rules.json. Auditor will verify these were correctly applied.

code_sections.description: Key code sections for auditor review. Include created/modified sections.
code_sections.$comment: Don't include entire files if large. Focus on sections relevant to rules and patterns.

file.description: File path relative to project root.

start_line.description: Starting line number of the section.

end_line.description: Ending line number of the section.

content.description: The actual code content of this section.
content.$comment: Include enough context for auditor to verify rules. For new files, can include entire content if small (<100 lines).

description.description: Brief description of what this section does.
description.$comment: Helps auditor understand intent. Example: 'DTO class with @Expose decorators'

implementation_notes.description: Free-form notes about the implementation. Include decisions, trade-offs, or anything the auditor should know.
implementation_notes.$comment: Write clearly. Mention any deviations from completion_guide and why.

dependencies_added.description: New dependencies or imports added during implementation.
dependencies_added.$comment: Helps track what new packages or modules were introduced.

name.description: Name of the dependency or import.

tests_written.description: Tests written as part of this implementation.
tests_written.$comment: Optional but encouraged. Include if objective mentioned tests.

audit_finding.description: A compliance finding from the auditor. Used in audit response, not in change_manifest itself.

rule_id.description: ID of the rule that was violated.

severity.description: Severity: error (must fix), warning (should fix), info (optional).

expected.description: What the code should look like.

found.description: What was actually found in the code.
